articles.title: Pound Sterling Price News and Forecast: GBP/USD rises toward 1.3500
articles.expand: The British Pound (GBP) strengthens against the US Dollar (USD) on Monday, with GBP/USD climbing toward the 1.3500 mark as improving optimism surrounding a possible US-Iran agreement boosts market sentiment and weighs on the Greenback.

articles.title: The Strait of Hormuz is to be opened in 30 days, Oil down 7% – WTI Technical analysis
articles.expand: WTI Oil Update: Oil is in an absolute freefall, tumbling 7% during a thin holiday session to retest early May lows. Driven entirely by President Trump's blockbuster announcement projecting a full reopening of the Strait of Hormuz within 30 days, explore our in-depth technical analysis of crude's critical structural pivot zones.

articles.title: US and Iran plan to open the Strait of Hormuz in about 30 days
articles.expand: More chatter in a fluid situation: Plan is to open the Strait of the Hormuz in around 30 days. Mines would be cleared No tolls on ships going through Ceasefire would be extended for 60 days. The nuclear part of the deal and the rescinding of sanctions are more challeging items that would need to be negotiated BEFORE there is increased hostilities. ...

articles.title: Australian Dollar Price Forecast: Recovery now targets 0.7200
articles.expand: The Aussie Dollar’s uptrend seems to have met some decent resistance in the 0.7270-0.7280 band so far, with AUD/USD still looking for a strong catalyst to extend its current recovery and confront the yearly peaks.

articles.title: Canadian Dollar firms against weaker US Dollar despite pressure from falling Oil prices
articles.expand: USD/CAD trades on the back foot on Monday, snapping a four-day winning streak as markets monitor evolving developments surrounding ongoing US-Iran negotiations. At the time of writing, the pair is trading around 1.3803 after easing from an intraday high near 1.3820.

articles.title: Crude oil extends lower and stalls the fall at a trend line target
articles.expand: Crude oil moved sharply lower today on hopes for a diplomatic solution to the conflict in Iran — or at least progress toward a framework for peace through a Memorandum of Agreement. The selloff pushed the price down to a low of $90.32, where buyers stepped in near an upward-sloping trend line connecting the late-February low to the April low. Holdi...

articles.title: Gold climbs 1% as hopes for US-Iran deal pressure Oil and the US Dollar
articles.expand: Gold (XAU/USD) rebounds sharply on Monday as hopes for a US-Iran deal to end the war in the Middle East and reopen the Strait of Hormuz weigh on the US Dollar (USD) and Oil prices. At the time of writing, XAU/USD is trading around $4,572, up 1.40% on the day.

articles.title: GBP/JPY Price Forecast: Bulls test 214.50 resistance as upside momentum improves
articles.expand: GBP/JPY trades with a positive bias on Monday as the British Pound (GBP) outperforms the Japanese Yen (JPY) amid improving market sentiment surrounding a potential US-Iran deal. At the time of writing, the cross is trading around 214.52, up 0.30% on the day.

articles.title: USDCHF bouncing off swing level support. 100 day MA remains a key target at 0.7839
articles.expand: The USDCHF extended to the downside today after finally breaking below the key 100-day moving average at 0.78393, a level that had helped hold support on Friday. Once below, downside momentum accelerated and pushed the pair down toward the May 14 swing low at 0.7807, where buyers stepped back in and stalled the fall. The bounce from that support le...

articles.title: Japanese Yen struggles for traction despite weaker US Dollar amid elevated Energy costs
articles.expand: USD/JPY consolidates on Monday after opening the week with a bearish gap as the US Dollar (USD) comes under pressure amid growing optimism that the United States (US) and Iran are moving closer toward a deal that could eventually reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

articles.title: Petrol Tops Rs 100 Across Most Cities, Diesel Nears Rs 100 As Prices Raised For Fourth Time
articles.expand: NEW DELHI, May 25: Petrol prices were raised by Rs 2.61 a litre and diesel by Rs 2.71 on Monday, the fourth increase in less than two weeks that extended a delayed pass-through of soaring global crude oil costs triggered by the Iran conflict. The latest increase took cumulative hikes since May 15 to nearly Rs 7.5 per litre. In Delhi, petrol prices ...

articles.title: Australian Dollar rises as improving sentiment outweighs decreasing RBA rate hike odds
articles.expand: AUD/USD holds ground after two days of losses, trading around 0.7170 during the early European hours on Monday. The pair appreciates as the Australian Dollar (AUD) receives support from fading safe-haven demand in anticipation of the United States (US)-Iran accord.

articles.title: EUR/USD Weekly Forecast: Awaiting more war-related headlines and central banks
articles.expand: The EUR/USD pair fell for a second consecutive week, settling not far above a fresh multi-week low of 1.1576. War-related headlines kept driving financial markets, coupled with mounting speculation that the United States (US) Federal Reserve (Fed) will deliver a rate hike before the year is over.
